characters.name: Catherine Bennet
characters.description: Catherine is the second youngest of the five sisters, at age 17. While not as strong-willed as her sister, Lydia, the two sisters together tear the mother's nerves into shreds. She is approximately seventeen years of age. According to Jane Austen's relatives, she revealed to them that Kitty later married a clergyman near Derbyshire. Kitty is described (in the novel) shorter than Lydia and prettier than Mary. In the movie she has light skin, brown eyes, an oval face shape, petite lips and dimples. She likes to wear her hair similar to Lydia in curls.

characters.name: Charlotte Lucas
characters.description: Charlotte Lucas is the daughter of Mr and Mrs Lucas. She is currently married to Mr Collins. Charlotte is strong, steady, and good. She genuinely cares for Elizabeth Bennet and takes joy in the prospect of Eliza’s high advancement through marriage with Mr.Darcy. Her father’s life in trade, politics and high society has provided her a wide exposure that amplifies her understanding.

characters.name: Colonel Fitzwilliam
characters.description: Colonel Fitzwilliamis a character fromPride and Prejudice. He is friends withElizabeth Bennetand isFitzwilliam Darcy's cousin. He is the second son of Lord and Lady Matlock, so chose the army as profession due to the fact he would not inherit the earldom. He is thought to be around 30 years of age.

characters.name: Elizabeth Bennet
characters.description: Elizabeth Bennet, later Elizabeth Darcy, is the main protagonist in the whole story. At twenty years of age, she is the second daughter in the Bennet family. Her siblings areJane,Kitty,Mary, andLydia. She once refused a proposal for marriage by her cousin,Mr. Collins, who ended up later marrying her best friend,Charlotte Lucas, instead. She eventually marriesFitzwilliam Darcy. Elizabeth Bennet is a headstrong, witty, logical, smart and exceptionally intelligent woman for her age. Elizabeth's strong-willed and intellectual personality is uncommon amongst women of that time and her tender age. She is not afraid to stand up for what she believes in, though she is not rude. Despite that, her wit and intelligence bring her closer to her father, Mr. Bennet, and particularly to her sister, Jane, who represents a compliment  to her personality. Her main antagonists are Lady Catherine De Bourgh, the Bingley sisters, Mrs. Hurst, and (later) Mr. Wickham. During the story, when she visits her best friend, Charlotte, and they are all invited to the house of Lady Catherine De Bourgh, Lady Catherine asks her many questions about her family. Because Elizabeth gave her opinions "so decidedly," Lady Catherine asks her her age. She answers that she "is not one and twenty," meaning she is not yet twenty-one years of age ("one and twenty" being an older way of saying "twenty-one"; cf. German, "einundzwanzig" (lit. "one and twenty")), making her about two years older than her sister, Mary. Elizabeth Bennet is a headstrong, witty, logical, smart and exceptionally intelligent woman for her age. Elizabeth's strong-willed and intellectual personality is uncommon amongst women of that time and her tender age. She is not afraid to stand up for what she believes in, though she is not rude. Despite that, her wit and intelligence bring her closer to her father, Mr. Bennet, and particularly to her sister, Jane, who represents a compliment  to her personality. Her main antagonists are Lady Catherine De Bourgh, the Bingley sisters, Mrs. Hurst, and (later) Mr. Wickham.

characters.name: Fitz Williams
characters.description: Fitz Williamsis a character fromThe Lizzie Bennet Diaries. He informedLizzie BennetthatWilliam Darcybroke upJaneandBing Lee, and he was responsible for introducing the videos toGigi Darcy.

characters.name: Fitz Williams
characters.description: Fitz Williamsis a character fromThe Lizzie Bennet Diaries. He informedLizzie BennetthatWilliam Darcybroke upJaneandBing Lee, and he was responsible for introducing the videos toGigi Darcy.

characters.name: Fitzwilliam Darcy
characters.description: Colin Firth (1995)  Fitzwilliam Darcyis a wealthy twenty-eight-year-old man. He is very fond and protective of his younger sister,Georgiana Darcy. He owns Pemberley and is best friends withCharles Bingley.Despite her lower class, he eventually marriesElizabeth Bennet, with whom he fell in love at the start of the novel. Mr. Darcy was found at first to be very proud, though later he admits that it was just because of his shyness. He is also said to have some prejudice, though he openly said that he tries not to. Darcy seems to try to do the best for his friends and himself. This is shown when he split up Mr. Bingley and Jane Bennet, thinking that Jane did not love Mr. Bingley. However, like the act previously mentioned, he failed to see the consequences. His good opinion, once lost, is lost forever.

characters.name: George Wickham
characters.description: George Wickhamis a young man in his mid to late 20's. He was the son of the late Mr Darcy's steward. His father died which Mr. Darcy told him as his second son. He is roughly around the same age asFitzwilliam Darcy. During the novel, he manages to seduce everyone around him with his polite and cheery manners, making him a true Austen villain. All the ladies love his looks, especially Elizabeth and Lydia. He manages to entrance Elizabeth by feeding into her dislike of Mr. Darcy until she learns the truth about Mr. Wickham's past and begins to doubt his intentions. Unfortunately, Elizabeth does not warn her family about the real Mr. Wickham, and is thus unable to save Lydia from disgrace (Lydia and Wickham were conducting very scandalous affairs that came to light when Lydia went to Brighton). He and Lydia later marry, but do not live in marital bliss, as they are always out of money and always have to look for work. George Wickham is described as handsome and charismatic that attracts the attention of all women including Elizabeth and Lydia. He is a very skilled liar as he was able to make Elizabeth dislike Darcy more if it hadn't been for her aunt and Darcy himself who told her the truth of Wickham's past. Also, he pretended to love Lydia fully aware that he has no intentions of marrying her until Darcy intervened and bribed him to marry Lydia to save the Bennet's honor. characters.description: George Wickhamis a character fromThe Lizzie Bennet Diaries.Lizzie Bennethas a romantic fling with him at one point in time, and isWilliam Darcy's enemy. George Wickham is based off ofGeorge WickhamfromPride and Prejudice.

characters.name: Georgiana Darcy
characters.description:
George Bingley
( supposedly) Georgiana Darcyis the younger sister'sMr. Darcy, and she is the friend ofElizabeth Bennet. Georgiana Darcy is the younger sister ofFitzwilliam Darcy. She is fifteen years old at the beginning of the story but later turns sixteen sometime within the storyline. She is portrayed as an innocent girl who loves the piano and shares a close bond with her brother (because of age, she may be looked up to him as sort of a father figure as well)

characters.name: Jane Bennet
characters.description: Fitzwilliam Darcy (brother-in-law),George Wickham (brother-in-law) Jane Bennet, later Jane Bingley, is the eldest daughter of Mr. and Mrs. Bennet, and thus the older sister ofElizabeth,Mary,Kitty, andLydia Bennet. She falls in love withCharles Bingley, and they later marry. She is twenty-two years old. She wears long skirts and corsets You never see a fault in anybody. All the world are good and agreeable in your eyes. I never heard you speak ill of a human being in your life. Jane, who is not as intellectual as her younger sister, Elizabeth, is seen as a very sweet, kind-hearted, gentle and compassionate young woman who desires to see only the best in people. She and Elizabeth share a close kinship because their personalities are quite complementary and because Elizabeth is the most mature of all of Jane's younger sisters.

characters.name: Lydia Bennet
characters.description: Lydia Bennet is the youngest of the five Bennet sisters. She is fifteen early in the story, but later turns sixteen. She is constantly competing with her sisterKitty, but it often shows how close they are as siblings. Lydia ran away withGeorge Wickhamafter being led to believe he would marry her, when he really had no intention of doing so. However, the pair were made to marry by Mr. Darcy, who acted in secret to help protect Elizabeth and the rest of the family's reputation. Lydia is strong-willed and frivolous, and often causes a scene. As the baby of the family, she is spoiled by her mother. However, she is the least dear to her father, who makes sarcastic remarks on hers and Kitty's naive and silly personalities. Her best friends are her sister Kitty and Mrs. Forster, who is mentioned often, but never seen in the novel. She loves dancing, shopping, and being a socialite. It has been speculated that this is Jane Austen's way of pointing out the characteristics of some of the naive debutantes in her era and satirizing them. characters.name: Mr. Bennet
characters.description: Mr. Bennet played by Donald Sutherland Mr. Bennet is a married gentlemen with his wifeMrs. Bennetand five daughters, but no son to continue his heir. This causes his wife to worry and their estate to fall into hands of a distant male relative whom may not take to heart in his will and wishes for his estate. Because Mr Bennet has no immediate male heir, the estate is entailed upon his next closest male relative. While his current heir is his distant second cousin,Mr. William Collins, before his death, if one of his daughters should be able to present him with a grandson, said-grandson would then become the new heir of the entailment over a distant cousin, by virtue of being his closest living male blood relative (this is likely why Mr. Collins' late father, Mr. Collins Sr., before his death, urged his son to 'mend the rift' with the Bennets; if his son were to be the husband of one of Mr. Bennet's daughters, it would reinforce Collins' claim to Longbourn, and furthermore if he were to be the father of said-grandson). Mr. Bennet is a gentlemen of few words. Often times throughout the story, he portrays a lot of reluctance towards his wife,Mrs. Bennet. Other times, he genuinely shows that he cares for his wife.

characters.name: Mrs. Forster
characters.description: Harriet Forstermost referred to asMrs. Forsteris the wife of Colonel Forster. She became friends withLydia Bennetand invited her as her companion to Brighton. But during their stay, Lydia ran off withGeorge Wickhamleaving only a note for Mrs. Forster.

characters.name: Mrs. Reynolds
characters.description: Mrs. Reynoldsis the housekeeper of Pemberley. It is implied that she took care of bothFitzwilliam Darcy(now her master) and his sister,Georgiana, when they were younger. Mrs. Reynolds received Elizabeth and her aunt and uncle when they visited Pemberley. She spoke aboutWickhamand more at-length about Darcy and Georgiana; it is her account that furthered Lizzy's reconsideration of Darcy's character. There is little known about Mrs. Reynolds' life outside of her life as the housekeeper at Pemberley, a job that she apparently took on when Darcy was four years old. "... I am afraid he has turned out very wild."

characters.name: Ricky Collins
characters.description: Ricky Collinsis a character fromThe Lizzie Bennet Diaries. He was a childhood friend ofLizzie Bennet, and isCharlotte Lu'sbusiness partner.

characters.name: Ricky Collins
characters.description: Ricky Collinsis a character fromThe Lizzie Bennet Diaries. He was a childhood friend ofLizzie Bennet, and isCharlotte Lu'sbusiness partner.

characters.name: William Collins
characters.description: Mr. Collins played by Tom Hollander Mr. Bennet's distant cousin. He marries Elizabeth Bennet's good friend and neighbor,Charlotte Lucas, and it is with them that Eliza stays when she goes to visit Kent. Mr. Collins is a cousin of Mr. Bennet. He is twenty five years of age and holds a profession as a clergyman. The Longbourn estate is entailed on him, and so he will receive it when Mr. Bennet dies. He once proposed to Elizabeth Bennet, but she refused; and he later married Charlotte Lucas. He greatly respects his patroness Lady Catherine de Bourgh.
